topic: part-to-part and part-to-whole ratios, word problem
latex:
A certain recipe for fruit salad calls for $3$ cups of pears, $2$ cups of bananas, and $1$ cup of watermelons. Write a three-part ratio representing the mix. Then write a part-to-whole ratio of watermelons to the total fruit salad. Finally, write a fraction in simplest form to express the proportion of watermelons to the whole.

watermelons : pears : bananas = \_\_\_\_ : \_\_\_\_ : \_\_\_\_

watermelons : fruit salad = \_\_\_\_ : \_\_\_\_

Proportion of watermelons (out of whole) = \_\_\_\_
